conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me first review the eligibility rules so I can ask you the right questions.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for your patience. I've reviewed the Child Benefit eligibility rules. To help you, I need to ask you some questions.

First, do you have any children you'd like to claim Child Benefit for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do. I have one child - my son Alex. I'd like to find out if I can claim Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to ask you a few questions.

First, how old is Alex?

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Since Alex is 21 years old, I need to understand his current situation. 

Is Alex in any approved education or training?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not in any education or training at the moment. He's actually working full-time at a warehouse.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. I have a few more questions to ensure I have a complete picture.

Do you live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do. I live in Leeds.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you. Just to confirm - does Alex live with you, or does he live elsewhere?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. Let me assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Based on what you've told me:
- Alex is 21 years old
- He is not in approved education or training
- He is working full-time
- You live in the UK
- Alex lives with you

I now have enough information to make a determination about your eligibility.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Assessment Complete**

I'm sorry, but you are **not eligible** for Child Benefit for Alex.

**The reason:** Child Benefit is available for children under 16, or for young people under 20 if they remain in approved education or training. Since Alex is 21 years old and is not in approved education or training, he exceeds the maximum age limit for Child Benefit eligibility.

Child Benefit would have stopped when Alex turned 20 (if he was in approved education or training at that point) or when he turned 16 and left education/training, whichever came first.

If you have any other questions or if your circumstances change, please feel free to ask.

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 21 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 21, which is 20 or over
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child works 24+ hours/week and is not in approved education
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child receives qualifying benefits (e.g. UC, ESA) in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 21, which is 20 or over; Child works 24+ hours/week and is not in approved education; Child receives qualifying benefits (e.g. UC, ESA) in their own right
circumstances: Child is 21, which is 20 or over
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child works 24+ hours/week and is not in approved education
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child receives qualifying benefits (e.g. UC, ESA) in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 21 years old and is not in approved education or training. According to the Child Benefit eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16. For children aged 16 or over, you'll normally still qualify if your child is under 20 and they stay in approved education or training. Since Alex is 21 years old and not in approved education or training, he does not meet the age criteria for Child Benefit eligibility.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex because he is 21 years old and not in approved education or training. Child Benefit is only available for children under 16, or for young people under 20 who remain in approved education or training. Alex exceeds the maximum age limit for Child Benefit.

Since Alex is 21 years old and not in approved education or training, he does not meet the age criteria for Child Benefit eligibility.

Child Benefit is only available for children under 16, or for young people under 20 who remain in approved education or training. Alex exceeds the maximum age limit for Child Benefit.
